findings.title: Email templates duplicated in two locations
findings.why_it_matters: src/lib/compliance/emailTemplates.ts and supabase/functions/_shared/emailTemplates.ts are identical. Any content update (subject line, branding, legal text) must be made in both files — easy to miss. This is a maintenance trap.
findings.suggested_fix: The plan explicitly chose this design to avoid Deno URL import issues. The correct long-term fix is a shared package or a build step. For now, add a prominent comment at the top of both files: '// SYNC: This file is duplicated at [other path]. Keep changes in sync.'
evidence: Any branding change requires two PRs or careful review

findings.title: Unsafe type casting instead of discriminated union narrowing
findings.why_it_matters: Pattern `(result as { skipped?: boolean }).skipped` bypasses TypeScript's type narrowing and can mask type errors if SendEmailResult union changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Use discriminated union narrowing: `if (!result.sent) { const r = result; if (!('skipped' in r && r.skipped)) { console.error(..., 'error' in r ? r.error : 'unknown') } }`
evidence: SendEmailResult is a discriminated union — can be narrowed safely
